chief_complaint: A 22-year-old woman presented with hematochezia and significant anemia.
history: Meckel's diverticulum is a congenital anomaly of the gastrointestinal tract, occurring in approximately 2% of the general population. This patient's case highlights a rare presentation of Meckel's diverticulum without ectopic gastric mucosa, which is uncommon in adults and usually causes bleeding due to ulceration from ectopic gastric mucosa.
exam: Vital signs: not provided. Physical examination findings: not provided.
labs: Initial endoscopic evaluation and contrast-enhanced computed tomography failed to identify the bleeding source.
correct_diagnosis: Meckel's diverticulum without ectopic gastric mucosa
differentials: Meckel's diverticulum without ectopic gastric mucosa
correct_ranking: Meckel's diverticulum without ectopic gastric mucosa
